method_atom: An equivalence-of-descriptions (duality) proof that embeds a system into a larger symmetry structure and then integrates out the extra degrees of freedom to show it is exactly equivalent to a superficially different dual formulation. The derivation hinges on an inversion duality that maps a scale/coupling parameter to its reciprocal, together with the nonperturbative generation of an effective potential by localized topological excitations (defects) of the enlarged theory.
object_atom: Two-dimensional supersymmetric sigma models on Kahler manifolds — including local and global Calabi-Yau manifolds and manifolds with positive first Chern class (with action deformations by holomorphic isometries) — realized as a gauged linear sigma model and shown equivalent to a Landau-Ginzburg theory of Toda type, thereby proving mirror symmetry.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the embed-into-larger-symmetry / integrate-out proof of duality using reciprocal-parameter inversion and defect-generated effective potentials; the domain-specific part is the 1+1D supersymmetric Kahler/Calabi-Yau sigma models and their gauged-linear-to-Landau-Ginzburg correspondence.
